source.title: Guidelines on the management of abnormal liver blood tests
source.population: Children and adults with abnormal liver blood tests, with recommendation-specific adult and paediatric differences.
source.setting: Primary and secondary care assessment of abnormal liver blood tests.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected result-context, history, adult liver-aetiology-screen, and clinician-referral documentation. The BSG resource page was last reviewed in 2021 with review due in 2025; UAE pathways and patient-specific interpretation require clinician review.

exact_location.heading: Recommendation 1 — standard initial liver blood-test panel

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician review of bilirubin and standard liver blood-test components; it does not provide patient-specific interpretation or diagnosis.

items.rationale: Exact phrase located in inspected full source.

items.final_wording: Supports age, hepatitis-risk context, jaundice, abdominal pain, weight loss, pruritus, comorbidity, prescribed or non-prescribed drug history, travel or exposure context, and alcohol history.

exact_location.heading: Recommendation 1 — standard initial liver blood-test panel

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician review of bilirubin and standard liver blood-test components; it does not provide patient-specific interpretation or diagnosis.

items.rationale: Supports mapped clinician review of liver blood tests without interpretation.

source.title: Guidelines on the management of abnormal liver blood tests

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 2–3 — prior results and clinical context

source.evidence_paraphrase: Requires review of previous results, past medical history, current condition, the specific abnormal analyte, and clinical context rather than inferring significance from magnitude alone.

items.rationale: Supports mapped result type, prior-result comparison, trend, and clinical-context review while prohibiting significance inference from magnitude alone.

items.final_wording: Supports age, hepatitis-risk context, jaundice, abdominal pain, weight loss, pruritus, comorbidity, prescribed or non-prescribed drug history, travel or exposure context, and alcohol history.

source.title: Guidelines on the management of abnormal liver blood tests

source.setting: Primary and secondary care assessment of abnormal liver blood tests.

exact_location.heading: Response to abnormal liver blood tests — clinical history

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports age, hepatitis-risk context, jaundice, abdominal pain, weight loss, pruritus, comorbidity, prescribed or non-prescribed drug history, travel or exposure context, and alcohol history.

items.rationale: Supports mapped alcohol, medicine or supplement, hepatitis-risk, jaundice, abdominal-pain, weight-loss, and pruritus history fields.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-reviewed abdominal ultrasound and hepatitis B or C testing in the recommendation-qualified adult liver-aetiology-screen context; investigation choice remains clinician-led.

source.title: Guidelines on the management of abnormal liver blood tests
source.population: Children and adults with abnormal liver blood tests, with recommendation-specific adult and paediatric differences.
source.setting: Primary and secondary care assessment of abnormal liver blood tests.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected result-context, history, adult liver-aetiology-screen, and clinician-referral documentation. The BSG resource page was last reviewed in 2021 with review due in 2025; UAE pathways and patient-specific interpretation require clinician review.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 4–6 — liver aetiology screen
exact_location.locator: PDF pages 1 and 10; lines 68–86 and 899 onward

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-reviewed abdominal ultrasound and hepatitis B or C testing in the recommendation-qualified adult liver-aetiology-screen context; investigation choice remains clinician-led.

items.rationale: Supports mapped clinician-reviewed adult ultrasound and viral-hepatitis fields in recommendation-qualified contexts.

items.final_wording: Supports clinician-led further investigation or referral based on synthetic failure, suspicious symptoms or signs, analyte pattern, persistence, risk factors, and clinical context without automatically assigning urgency.

source.setting: Primary and secondary care assessment of abnormal liver blood tests.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected result-context, history, adult liver-aetiology-screen, and clinician-referral documentation. The BSG resource page was last reviewed in 2021 with review due in 2025; UAE pathways and patient-specific interpretation require clinician review.

exact_location.heading: Response pathway and Recommendation 10
exact_location.locator: PDF pages 2, 9, and 12; figure 1 and Recommendation 10

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinician-led further investigation or referral based on synthetic failure, suspicious symptoms or signs, analyte pattern, persistence, risk factors, and clinical context without automatically assigning urgency.

added_items.rationale: Supports clinician-arranged further testing and clinician-decided referral documentation without automatic urgency.
